content.problemStatement:
A flower shop has NNN flowers for sale. These flowers are arranged in a row from left to right.

The iii-th flower has color CiC_iCi​.

You would like to buy some contiguous range of these flowers.

Specifically, you've shortlisted MMM ranges of flowers.

The iii-th range starts at index LiL_iLi​ and ends at index RiR_iRi​, and includes both endpoints.

For two ranges iii and jjj (1≤i,j≤M1 \leq i, j \leq M1≤i,j≤M) of flowers, define f(i,j)f(i, j)f(i,j) to be the number of **distinct colors** of flowers that appear in the iii-th range, but not in the jjj-th range.

Note that f(i,j)f(i, j)f(i,j) might not equal f(j,i)f(j, i)f(j,i).

Define

Di=∑j=1Mf(i,j)D_i = \sum_{j=1}^M f(i, j)Di​=j=1∑M​f(i,j)Find the values D1,D2,…,DMD_1, D_2, \ldots, D_MD1​,D2​,…,DM​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM — the number of flowers for sale and the number of ranges, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,…,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1​,C2​,…,CN​ — the colors of the flowers.
The next MMM lines describe the ranges chosen by you. The iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line MMM space-separated integers: D1,D2,…,DMD_1, D_2, \ldots, D_MD1​,D2​,…,DM​.
constraints: The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases both won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.
